title: Multi-coil base pad with angled structure
abstract: An apparatus for wirelessly transferring charging power is provided. The apparatus comprises a first coil, as second coil, a ferrite structure positioned on one side of the first coil and the second coil. The ferrite structure includes a first angled surface at an outer side of the first coil and a second angled surface at an outer side of the second coil. The ferrite structure further includes a substantially flat surface at an inner side of the first coil and an inner side of the second coil. The first angled surface and the second angled surface are at an angle to the flat surface.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS; 1. An apparatus for wirelessly transferring charging power, comprising: a first coil; a second coil; and a ferrite structure positioned on one side of the first coil and the second coil, the ferrite structure comprising a first angled surface at an outer side of the first coil and a second angled surface at an outer side of the second coil, the ferrite structure further comprising a substantially flat surface at an inner side of the first coil and an inner side of the second coil, wherein the first angled surface and the second angled surface are at an angle to the flat surface. 2. The apparatus of claim 1 , wherein the first coil and the second coil form at least a portion of a double D coil configuration. 3. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the inner side of the first coil is adjacent to the inner side of the second coil, and wherein a distance between the inner side of the first coil and the inner side of the second coil is less than a distance between the outer side of the first coil and the outer side of the second coil. 4. The apparatus of claim 1 , wherein the first coil and the second coil are at least partially overlapping. 5. The apparatus of claim 1, further comprising a driver configured to drive each of the first coil and the second coil such that current on the inner side of the first coil travels in the same direction as current on the inner side of the second coil, wherein the first coil and the second coil are substantially coplanar, and wherein the first coil is adjacent to the second coil. 6. The apparatus of claim 5, wherein the first coil and the second coil are configured to generate flux that exits from an opening of the first coil and enters at an opening of the second coil. 7. The apparatus of claim 1, further comprising a driver configured to generate a current in each of the first coil and the second coil to generate a magnetic field for wireless power transfer. 8. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the first angled surface and the second angled surface face toward an inner side of the first coil and an inner side of the second coil. 9. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the outer side of the first coil and the outer side of the second coil are substantially parallel to the flat surface. 10. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the outer side of the first coil is substantially parallel to the first angled surface, and wherein the outer side of the second coil is substantially parallel to the second angled surface. 11. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the inner side of the first coil and the inner side of the second coil are substantially parallel to the flat surface. 12. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the angle is between 30 degrees and 60 degrees. 13. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the ferrite structure, the first coil and the second coil are buried below or flush mounted in a driving surface. 14. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the ferrite structure extends laterally beyond edges of the first coil and the second coil. 15. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the ferrite structure forms a ferrite border along an entire perimeter of the first coil and the second coil. 16. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein at least a portion of the first coil and second coil are recessed into the ferrite structure. 17. A method for wirelessly transferring charging power, comprising: driving each of a first coil and a second coil with a current to generate an alternating magnetic field; and wirelessly transferring charging power to at least one wireless power receiver via the alternating magnetic field, wherein: 

 the first coil and the second coil are positioned on one side of a ferrite structure, the ferrite structure comprises a first angled surface at an outer side of the first coil and a second angled surface at an outer side of the second coil, the ferrite structure further comprises a flat surface at an inner side of the first coil and an inner side of the second coil, and the first angled surface and the second angled surface are at an angle to the flat surface. 18. The method of claim 17, wherein the first coil and the second coil form at least a portion of a double D coil configuration. 19. The method of claim 17, wherein driving each of the first coil and the second coil comprises driving each of the first coil and the second coil such that current on the inner side of the first coil travels in the same direction as current on the inner side of the second coil, wherein the first coil and the second coil are coplanar, and wherein the first coil is adjacent to the second coil. 20. The method of claim 19, further comprising generating flux that exits from an opening of the first coil and enters at an opening of the second coil. 21. The method of claim 17, wherein the outer side of the first coil and the outer side of the second coil are substantially parallel to the flat surface. 22. The method of claim 17, wherein the outer side of the first coil is substantially parallel to the first angled surface, and wherein the outer side of the second coil is substantially parallel to the second angled surface. 23. The method of claim 17, wherein the inner side of the first coil and the inner side of the second coil are substantially parallel to the flat surface. 24. The method of claim 17, wherein the angle is between 30 degrees and 60 degrees. 25. An apparatus for wirelessly transferring charging power, comprising: means for generating an alternating magnetic field; and 

 means for wirelessly transferring charging power to at least one wireless power receiver via the alternating magnetic field, wherein the means for wirelessly transferring charging power comprises a ferrite structure, the ferrite structure comprising a first angled surface at a first outer side of the ferrite structure and a second angled surface at a second outer side of the ferrite structure, the ferrite structure further comprising a flat surface at an inner side of the ferrite structure, and wherein the first angled surface and the second angled surface are at an angle to the flat surface. 26. The apparatus of claim 25, further comprising means for generating flux that exits from one area of the ferrite structure and enters at another area of the ferrite structure. 27. The apparatus of claim 25, wherein the angle is between 30 degrees and 60 degrees. 28. An apparatus for wirelessly transferring charging power, comprising: a first coil having a plurality of turns wound around a first opening, the first coil having a first side and a second side; a second coil having a plurality of turns wound around a second opening, the second coil having a first side and a second side, the second coil being positioned adjacent to or partially overlapping the first coil, the first side of the first coil being positioned adjacent to and closer to the first side of the second coil than to the second side of the second coil, the plurality of turns of the first side of the first coil and the first side of the second coil being substantially co-planar to one another, the plurality of turns of the second side of the first coil and the second side of the first coil being at an angle relative to the first side of the first coil and the first side of the second coil; and a ferromagnetic layer positioned on one side of the first and second coils. 29. The apparatus of claim 28, wherein the angle is between 30 degrees and 60 degrees. 30. The apparatus of claim 28, further comprising a driver configured to drive each 

 of the first coil and the second coil such that current on the first side of the first coil travels in the same direction as current on the first side of the second coil.
